   - code_review/grader.py: detection_score, fix_score, quality_score, total
   - incident_response/grader.py: all 5 phase scores + overall_score
4. .space/ folder synced with identical changes for HuggingFace Space

Verified: All grader edge cases (empty inputs, perfect scores) return
values strictly in (0, 1) interval.

fix: derive cookie Secure flag from base URL, not NODE_ENV

When running in production over HTTP (e.g. LXC on local network),
NODE_ENV=production was setting the Secure cookie flag, causing the
browser to refuse sending the session cookie over plain HTTP. This
broke the OAuth state validation (state_mismatch error).

Now the Secure flag is set only when NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL starts
with https://, which correctly handles HTTP-in-production setups.

fix: shorten newsletter URL params to stay under Mailgun 1000-char tracking limit

Mailgun silently skips click-tracking for href values >= 1000 characters.
Our newsletter URLs with authToken JWT were ~1010-1087 chars, causing
only 7/24 links to be tracked (those with shorter paths).

Changes:
- newsletter_email -> ne (saves 14 chars)
- authToken -> at (saves 7 chars)
- Drop newsletter_autologin param (always 1, saves 24 chars)
- Drop newsletter_source param (always 'weekly', saves 26 chars)
- Drop subscriber_key param (redundant with email, saves 40 chars)
- Revert & encoding (not the root cause)
- Total savings: ~111 chars -> max URL now ~976 chars

Client-side (App.tsx, authService.ts, build-plugins) reads both old
and new param names for backwards compatibility with cached emails.

commit.message:
Rework ballscrew joint model to match physical design

Replace old slider-crank (A/B pivot on thigh/shank) with the accurate
model: motor on top of upper-leg tube drives a nut up/down the shaft;
nut connects through a slot to the shank at distance R_ARM from the
knee pivot.

Key changes:
- leg_kinematics.py: replace ballscrew_length/velocity with
  nut_position(), nut_lever_arm(), nut_velocity(), nut_stroke_for_range()
  using virtual-work relationship F = tau / (R_ARM * |sin theta|)
- force_calculator.py: rename functions (nut_force_required,
  motor_torque_from_nut_force, motor_speed_rpm, motor_power);
  add CARRIAGE_DRAG_N=0 parameter; add motor RPM/power outputs
- main.py: update summary table with nut stroke, lever arm, screw params
- pybullet_sim.py: import CARRIAGE_DRAG_N

Results: peak nut force ~774 N, motor torque ~0.69 N*m (5mm lead, 90% eff)
Nut stroke: 92.7 mm over working range, 37.6 mm during gait cycle.

Fix controller fighting user overrides

Last night the user manually warmed to -1 at 3:21 AM. After the 15-min
freeze expired, the controller marched the setting back from -3 to -9
over the next hour, completely ignoring the user's intent.

Three fixes:
1. Override freeze increased to 60 min (was 15 — too short)
2. Kill switch window increased to 5 min (was 20s — never triggered)
3. After freeze expires, controller respects user's value as a floor —
   it will never set colder than what the user chose. The floor persists
   for the rest of the night.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

consensus: fix TOCTOU race in validate_block_rewards causing false reward-injection rejects

Step 2 was calling the live tier_for_wallet (fresh registry read) while Step 5
used the all_infos snapshot captured at the start of validation.  A masternode
that gossip-registered between those two reads would be classified as 'known'
in Step 2 (keeping unknown_non_producer_paid == 0, so Step 5 ran) but absent
from all_infos, causing the is_known_masternode fallback in Step 5 to return
false and hard-reject a fully valid block.

Fix: replace the live tier_for_wallet call with snapshot_tier_for_wallet, a
closure that searches the already-captured all_infos slice.  Steps 2 and 5 now
use the same registry snapshot.  If a wallet is absent from the snapshot,
Step 2 increments unknown_non_producer_paid, which causes Step 5's strict
bitmap address check to be skipped (the correct lenient path — pool amounts
are still fully validated in Steps 2-4).

This was causing the node to stall at height 1092, rejecting block 1093 from
all peers including whitelisted nodes, with spurious 'possible reward injection'
warnings for legitimate Silver-pool recipient TIME15E85jKLtMsWax7oQVTHy8toXHHVauxj8H.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

EXP-1241-1250: gap-closing experiments with two breakthroughs

Key discoveries:
- EXP-1249: Patient similarity transfer +0.017 R², 10/11 wins ★★★★★
  Augment training with 2 most similar patients at 0.3 weight
  Data-scarce patients benefit most (j: +0.053, h: +0.034)
- EXP-1248: Quantile ensemble (q25/50/75) +0.014 R² ★★★★
  Averaging 3 quantile regressors beats MSE baseline
- EXP-1241: Naive ensemble doesn't reproduce EXP-1211's 0.781
  The build_enhanced_multi_horizon() mechanism is architecture-dependent
- EXP-1242: AR coefficients near zero at all horizons (30/60/90 min)
- EXP-1244: Exponential decay = zero effect (tree scale-invariance)
- EXP-1246: Stratified models always hurt (-0.026, 0/11 wins)
- EXP-1247: Error-weighted retraining marginal (-0.003)
- EXP-1250: Variance-stabilizing transforms hurt (log: -0.013)

Add CatArrayBatchedCopy_contig relative kernels (#1821)

https://github.com/intel/torch-xpu-ops/issues/1764
**Improvements**
- Fixed incorrect indexing behavior for channels-last non-contiguous cat
inputs.
- Aligned non-contiguous input metadata mapping with the same
logical-dimension semantics used by the output path.
- Preserved fast-path behavior for contiguous and channels-last
contiguous cases.
- Maintained replay-level throughput with slight overall improvement.

**Graph-Level Layout Coverage and Impact (Conv + Concat)**
- Before this fix, channels-last propagation was incomplete in the
conv+concat graph family.
- Cat-output channels-last coverage:
Overall across the 4 graph variants: 25% (1/4) -> 100% (target
deployment path).
Within channels-last-input graph variants: 33.3% (1/3) -> 100% (target
deployment path).
- Once the graph stays fully channels-last end-to-end, measured gains
versus mixed-layout states are:
Against channels_last_to_channel_first_cat:
conv mean: 266.33 us -> 148.58 us (1.79x faster, -44.2%)
cat mean: 101.55 us -> 65.44 us (1.55x faster, -35.6%)
Against channels_last_cat_then_channel_first_out:
conv mean: 298.30 us -> 148.58 us (2.01x faster, -50.2%)
cat mean: 122.86 us -> 65.44 us (1.88x faster, -46.7%)

**Baseline Performance**
Replay-based concat latency is effectively unchanged after the fix.
Aggregate geomean: 1.000x -> 1.004x.
Per-case movement is within a small range (approximately -1.4% to
+0.9%).
Conclusion: the correctness fix is performance-neutral at baseline
scale, with no observable regression.

Major eval quality improvements based on grounding truth case analysis

CSV display values:
- Use openpyxl cell number_format for accurate display (dates, decimals, thousands)
- Thousand separators preserved in CSV (e.g., 1,158 instead of 1158)
- Date columns formatted according to Excel cell format (date-only vs datetime)

Dimension boundary clarification:
- Relevance: completely redefined as 'is there irrelevant content?' not 'are requirements met?'
- Completeness: added boundary - does not judge sheet organization or formatting
- Formula Logic: do not mention redundant parentheses, IFERROR, or source file references
- All dims: source data characteristics and copied input values are not errors

Screenshots:
- Completeness now receives screenshots (confirm highlighting requirements)
- Relevance and Table Structure do not need screenshots (CSV display values sufficient)

Sheet Organization:
- Section detection added to data package (blank-row separator analysis)
- Scoring examples for single-sheet-with-sections (score 2 minimum)
- Do not require source file tabs in output

Table Structure:
- Frozen panes threshold: 20 rows OR 20 columns
- Thousand separator note: numbers <1000 naturally have no comma
- CSV artifact warnings for floating-point and datetime display

AI agent behavior:
- All prompts: do not penalize for hardcoded values copied from input files
- Formula Logic: VLOOKUP to source tabs not expected (source not in output)

commit.message:
Fix closure cell leaks, for-let force_local_let restore, and ClearLocalCells opcode

Three interrelated fixes for block-scoped closure/TDZ correctness:

1. Save/restore block_stmt_depth and force_local_let in all 6 function
   compilation paths (FnDecl, arrow, fn expr, async gen, gen, class ctor)
   so these states don't leak from enclosing blocks into nested functions.

2. Add ClearLocalCells opcode (110) to clean up stale closure cells between
   loop iterations and at block scope exits. Emitted in for-of, for-in,
   destructuring loops, and block statements. Prevents cross-iteration cell
   leaking at the top level.

3. Fix for-statement init clause to save/restore force_local_let instead of
   unconditionally resetting it to false. A for(let i...) inside a bare
   block (which sets force_local_let=true) was destroying the outer block's
   state, causing subsequent class declarations to emit DefineGlobal instead
   of SetLocal — leaving their pre-declared TDZ cells uninitialized.

4. Remove is_module_mode guard on MakeClosure top-level cell persistence
   so script-mode closures also get live bindings (not snapshots).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
